description:
Operating Area: Corsica, Marseille, Balearic Is., Italy, Sardinia

The Oyster 825 Champagne Hippy epitomizes opulence on the high seas, boasting a majestic 25-meter profile renowned for her unparalleled performance, graceful design, and abundant onboard features. With lavish lodgings for up to 6 guests across 3 sumptuous cabins, complemented by dedicated crew quarters, this masterpiece, conceived and crafted by Oyster Yachts, seamlessly harmonizes chic aesthetics with artisanal craftsmanship and luxurious design. 

Following an extensive and transformative refit, culminating in June 2023, this yacht has undergone a magnificent metamorphosis. She now stands as the zenith of luxury, ensuring paramount safety, contemporary style, and optimal functionality for those privileged enough to set sail aboard her.

crew.name: Christian Atkinson
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
From sailing with the Devon and Cornwall Autistic Trust to commanding prestigious charters and racing yachts, Christian brings a wealth of experience and professionalism to every program he joins. With a career spanning over 25 years and more than 100,000 sea miles, he has worked aboard more than 20 yachts, serving as captain or relief captain on 17 of them. His experience includes managing both private and charter programs across the Mediterranean, Caribbean, and Atlantic. He has overseen new builds, restorations, and successful regatta campaigns, including wins at Les Voiles de Saint Tropez and Regatta Royale de Cannes. Notable roles include captaining SY IDUNA, a 33.5m classic ketch, SY KIANDA, a Swan 68, and several Oyster yachts such as SY ALIKA and SY IRENE III. His longstanding commitment to excellence is evident in his attention to detail, operational knowledge, and ability to deliver memorable experiences to owners and guests.

Job Description
Christian is responsible for the safe and efficient operation of the yacht, managing navigation, compliance, maintenance, crew coordination, and guest satisfaction. He ensures adherence to safety procedures, legal regulations, and delivers high-standard charter and private cruising experiences. His leadership style is based on integrity, leading by example, and maintaining a detail-oriented and well-organized operation.

Licences and Qualifications
• MCA / RYA 200T Yacht Master Ocean Sail & Motor Commercial
• ENG1
• STCW
• AEC
• HELM
• PPR
• GMDSS GOC
• RNLI Lifeboat Crew Certificate
• Short Range Radio Certificate
• Seaman’s Discharge Book
• ICC Certificate
• Mini ISM
• SOP

Languages:
English

crew.name: Andrew Duncan
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Andrew is a skilled deckhand and engineer with over 20,000 nautical miles and several charter seasons to his name. His most recent role was as Deck Engineer aboard the classic 33m S/Y IDUNA, where he handled technical maintenance and supported deck operations for private charters and events. His previous experience includes deckhand and diver work with FIORDLAND EXPEDITIONS in New Zealand and skippering a private motor yacht in the UK. He has also been involved in restoration projects such as HMS PICKLE. In addition to his maritime career, Andrew worked for several years as a diagnostic radiographer across various healthcare settings, where he trained staff and managed trauma and outpatient imaging scenarios—skills which reinforce his calm, capable approach onboard. Outside of sailing his interests include diving, climbing and swimming. 

Job Description
Andrew is responsible for the upkeep and troubleshooting of all onboard systems, working closely with the captain to ensure the vessel remains safe, functional, and ready for service. His duties span preventative maintenance, deck operations, mooring and anchoring assistance, and supporting smooth day-to-day technical performance.

Licenses and Qualifications
• STCW (exp. 2030)
• ENG1 (exp. 2027)
• RYA Yachtmaster Offshore (Sail) with Ocean Theory
• MCA Approved Engine Course 1 & 2
• RYA Powerboat Level 2, Diesel Engine, Radar, VHF
• MROCP (Marine Radio Operators Certificate)
• Marine & Emergency First Aid, Mental Health First Aid
• BTEC Level 3 Engineering
• BSc (Hons) Diagnostic Radiography
• HCPC registered

crew.name: Maria Belén Jaleo
crew.bio:
Sail and Work Experience
Maria is an experienced stewardess with a strong hospitality background and growing culinary expertise. She has worked aboard yachts up to 50m in the Mediterranean, including S/Y IDUNA, M/Y FONERS, and S/Y ARESTEAS. Her roles have included both sole and second stewardess positions, and she has also taken on responsibilities as a sous chef aboard S/Y ARESTEAS and assisted the head chef on M/Y FONERS. In her most recent position aboard S/Y IDUNA, she was employed as sole stewardess, managing the entire interior. With a degree in Tourism and several seasons in the hospitality industry—including Michelin-listed restaurants—Maria brings refined service skills and a passion for guest care and cuisine.

Job Description
Maria is solely responsible for both the interior and galley. As sole stewardess, she manages all aspects of interior service including housekeeping, laundry, provisioning, and guest care. As sole chef, she plans and prepares all meals for guests and crew, offering fresh, seasonal, and well-presented cuisine. Her hospitality experience, and willingness to take on varied responsibilities make her a valuable asset to any team.

Licenses and Qualifications
• STCW Basic Safety Training
• ENG1 Seafarers Medical Certificate
• Food Hygiene Level II
• Bachelor’s Degree in Tourism – Mar del Plata National University
• Wine Course – UTHGRA (Argentinian Hospitality Trade Union)

Languages:
Spanish, English, Portuguese
